files.utils/security.py:
import hashlib
import secrets

def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
    hashed = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode()).hexdigest()
    return f"{salt}:{hashed}"

def verify_password(password: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
    salt, expected = hashed.split(":")
    actual = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode()).hexdigest()
    return actual == expected
files.tests/test_user.py:
import pytest
from models.user import User

def test_create_user():
    u = User("Alice", "alice@example.com", 30)
    assert u.name == "Alice"

def test_to_dict():
    u = User("Bob", "bob@example.com", 25)
    assert u.to_dict()["name"] == "Bob"

def test_age_type():
    u = User("Carol", "carol@example.com", 40)
    assert isinstance(u.age, int)

def test_invalid_email():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        User("Bad", "not-an-email", 25)

def test_invalid_age():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        User("Old", "old@example.com", 200)

files.tests/test_db.py:
import os
import pytest
from models.user import User
from db.user_repo import UserRepository
import db.connection as conn_mod

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def use_temp_db(tmp_path):
    conn_mod._DB_PATH = str(tmp_path / "test.db")
    yield

def test_save_and_find():
    repo = UserRepository()
    u = User("Alice", "alice@test.com", 30)
    repo.save(u)
    found = repo.find_by_email("alice@test.com")
    assert found is not None
    assert found.name == "Alice"

def test_find_missing():
    repo = UserRepository()
    assert repo.find_by_email("nobody@test.com") is None

def test_update_existing():
    repo = UserRepository()
    u = User("Alice", "alice@test.com", 30)
    repo.save(u)
    u2 = User("Alice Updated", "alice@test.com", 31)
    repo.save(u2)
    found = repo.find_by_email("alice@test.com")
    assert found.name == "Alice Updated"

files.db/user_repo.py:
from db.connection import get_connection, close_connection
from models.user import User

class UserRepository:
    def save(self, user: User) -> None:
        conn = get_connection()
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE, age INTEGER, password_hash TEXT)")
        conn.execute("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO users (name, email, age, password_hash) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                     (user.name, user.email, user.age, user.password_hash))
        conn.commit()
        close_connection(conn)

    def find_by_email(self, email: str):
        conn = get_connection()
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE, age INTEGER, password_hash TEXT)")
        row = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?", (email,)).fetchone()
        close_connection(conn)
        if row:
            u = User(name=row["name"], email=row["email"], age=row["age"])
            u.password_hash = row["password_hash"] or ""
            return u
        return None

files.tests/test_auth.py:
import pytest
import db.connection as conn_mod
from api.auth import register, login

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def use_temp_db(tmp_path):
    conn_mod._DB_PATH = str(tmp_path / "test.db")
    yield

def test_register_new_user():
    result = register("Alice", "alice@test.com", "pass123")
    assert result["success"] is True

def test_register_duplicate():
    register("Alice", "alice@test.com", "pass123")
    result = register("Alice2", "alice@test.com", "pass456")
    assert "error" in result

def test_login_success():
    register("Alice", "alice@test.com", "pass123")
    result = login("alice@test.com", "pass123")
    assert result["success"] is True

def test_login_wrong_password():
    register("Alice", "alice@test.com", "pass123")
    result = login("alice@test.com", "wrong")
    assert "error" in result

def test_login_missing_user():
    result = login("nobody@test.com", "pass")
    assert "error" in result

files.middleware/rate_limiter.py:
import time
from collections import defaultdict

class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, max_requests: int = 100, window_seconds: int = 60):
        self.max_requests = max_requests
        self.window_seconds = window_seconds
        self._requests: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)

    def check_rate_limit(self, ip: str) -> bool:
        """Return True if request is allowed, False if rate limited."""
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        # Clean old entries
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        if len(self._requests[ip]) >= self.max_requests:
            return False
        self._requests[ip].append(now)
        return True

    def reset_limits(self) -> None:
        """Clear all rate limit tracking."""
        self._requests.clear()

    def get_remaining(self, ip: str) -> int:
        """Return remaining requests for this IP in current window."""
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        return max(0, self.max_requests - len(self._requests[ip]))
files.tests/test_rate_limiter.py:
import time
from middleware.rate_limiter import RateLimiter

def test_allows_under_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_blocks_over_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=3, window_seconds=60)
    for _ in range(3):
        rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False

def test_different_ips_independent():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=2, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("2.2.2.2") is True

def test_reset_clears():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=1, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False
    rl.reset_limits()
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_remaining_count():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 5
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 4

files.middleware/rate_limiter.py:
import time
from collections import defaultdict
from utils.logger import get_logger

_log = get_logger("rate_limiter")

class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, max_requests: int = 100, window_seconds: int = 60):
        self.max_requests = max_requests
        self.window_seconds = window_seconds
        self._requests: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)

    def check_rate_limit(self, ip: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        if len(self._requests[ip]) >= self.max_requests:
            _log.warning(f"Rate limit hit for {ip}")
            return False
        self._requests[ip].append(now)
        return True

    def reset_limits(self) -> None:
        self._requests.clear()

    def get_remaining(self, ip: str) -> int:
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        return max(0, self.max_requests - len(self._requests[ip]))
files.tests/test_rate_limiter.py:
import time
from middleware.rate_limiter import RateLimiter

def test_allows_under_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_blocks_over_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=3, window_seconds=60)
    for _ in range(3):
        rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False

def test_different_ips_independent():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=2, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("2.2.2.2") is True

def test_reset_clears():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=1, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False
    rl.reset_limits()
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_remaining_count():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 5
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 4
files.utils/logger.py:
import logging
import sys

def get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger:
    """Return a configured logger with timestamp, level, name, message format."""
    logger = logging.getLogger(name)
    if not logger.handlers:
        handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
        formatter = logging.Formatter(
            "%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s",
            datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
        )
        handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        logger.addHandler(handler)
        logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    return logger
files.tests/test_logger.py:
from utils.logger import get_logger

def test_get_logger():
    log = get_logger("test")
    assert log.name == "test"
    assert len(log.handlers) > 0

def test_logger_singleton():
    log1 = get_logger("same")
    log2 = get_logger("same")
    assert log1 is log2
    assert len(log1.handlers) == 1

    assert u.name == "Alice"

def test_to_dict():
    u = User("Bob", "bob@example.com", 25)
    assert u.to_dict()["name"] == "Bob"

def test_age_type():
    u = User("Carol", "carol@example.com", 40)
    assert isinstance(u.age, int)

def test_invalid_email():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        User("Bad", "not-an-email", 25)

def test_invalid_age():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        User("Old", "old@example.com", 200)

import pytest
from models.user import User
from db.user_repo import UserRepository
import db.connection as conn_mod

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def use_temp_db(tmp_path):
    conn_mod._DB_PATH = str(tmp_path / "test.db")
    yield

def test_save_and_find():
    repo = UserRepository()
    u = User("Alice", "alice@test.com", 30)
    repo.save(u)
    found = repo.find_by_email("alice@test.com")
    assert found is not None
    assert found.name == "Alice"

def test_find_missing():
    repo = UserRepository()
    assert repo.find_by_email("nobody@test.com") is None

def test_update_existing():
    repo = UserRepository()
    u = User("Alice", "alice@test.com", 30)
    repo.save(u)
    u2 = User("Alice Updated", "alice@test.com", 31)
    repo.save(u2)
    found = repo.find_by_email("alice@test.com")
    assert found.name == "Alice Updated"

files.middleware/rate_limiter.py:
import time
from collections import defaultdict
from config.settings import settings
from utils.logger import get_logger

_log = get_logger("rate_limiter")

class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, max_requests: int = None, window_seconds: int = 60):
        self.max_requests = max_requests or settings.RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE
        self.window_seconds = window_seconds
        self._requests: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)

    def check_rate_limit(self, ip: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        if len(self._requests[ip]) >= self.max_requests:
            _log.warning(f"Rate limit hit for {ip}")
            return False
        self._requests[ip].append(now)
        return True

    def reset_limits(self) -> None:
        self._requests.clear()

    def get_remaining(self, ip: str) -> int:
        now = time.time()
        cutoff = now - self.window_seconds
        self._requests[ip] = [t for t in self._requests[ip] if t > cutoff]
        return max(0, self.max_requests - len(self._requests[ip]))
files.tests/test_rate_limiter.py:
import time
from middleware.rate_limiter import RateLimiter

def test_allows_under_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_blocks_over_limit():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=3, window_seconds=60)
    for _ in range(3):
        rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False

def test_different_ips_independent():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=2, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.1.1.1")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("2.2.2.2") is True

def test_reset_clears():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=1, window_seconds=60)
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is False
    rl.reset_limits()
    assert rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4") is True

def test_remaining_count():
    rl = RateLimiter(max_requests=5, window_seconds=60)
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 5
    rl.check_rate_limit("1.2.3.4")
    assert rl.get_remaining("1.2.3.4") == 4
files.utils/logger.py:
import logging
import sys

def get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger:
    """Return a configured logger with timestamp, level, name, message format."""
    logger = logging.getLogger(name)
    if not logger.handlers:
        handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
        formatter = logging.Formatter(
            "%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s",
            datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
        )
        handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        logger.addHandler(handler)
        logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    return logger

    assert log.name == "test"
    assert len(log.handlers) > 0

def test_logger_singleton():
    log1 = get_logger("same")
    log2 = get_logger("same")
    assert log1 is log2
    assert len(log1.handlers) == 1

files.utils/security.py:
import hashlib
import secrets

def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
    hashed = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode()).hexdigest()
    return f"{salt}:{hashed}"

def verify_password(password: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
    salt, expected = hashed.split(":")
    actual = hashlib.sha256((salt + password).encode()).hexdigest()
    return actual == expected
files.db/connection.py:
import sqlite3
from config.settings import settings

_DB_PATH = settings.get_db_path()

def get_connection() -> sqlite3.Connection:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(_DB_PATH)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    return conn

def close_connection(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
    conn.close()
files.db/user_repo.py:
from db.connection import get_connection, close_connection
from models.user import User

class UserRepository:
    def save(self, user: User) -> None:
        conn = get_connection()
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE, age INTEGER, password_hash TEXT)")
        conn.execute("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO users (name, email, age, password_hash) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                     (user.name, user.email, user.age, user.password_hash))
        conn.commit()
        close_connection(conn)

    def find_by_email(self, email: str):
        conn = get_connection()
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE, age INTEGER, password_hash TEXT)")
        row = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?", (email,)).fetchone()
        close_connection(conn)
        if row:
            u = User(name=row["name"], email=row["email"], age=row["age"])
            u.password_hash = row["password_hash"] or ""
            return u
        return None

files.tests/test_sessions.py:
import time
from api.sessions import create_session, validate_session, expire_session, active_session_count
import api.sessions as sess_mod

def test_create_and_validate():
    token = create_session(user_id=42)
    assert validate_session(token) == 42

def test_invalid_token():
    assert validate_session("bogus-token") is None

def test_expire_session():
    token = create_session(user_id=42)
    expire_session(token)
    assert validate_session(token) is None

def test_expired_by_time():
    token = create_session(user_id=42)
    # Manually set last_active to past
    sess_mod._sessions[token]["last_active"] = time.time() - 3600
    assert validate_session(token) is None

def test_session_count():
    sess_mod._sessions.clear()
    create_session(user_id=1)
    create_session(user_id=2)
    assert active_session_count() == 2
